title: Anjanvel Fort
tagline: Anjanvel Fort — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: Gopalgad Fort / Anjanvel Fort (Marathi: अंजनवेल चा किल्ला/ गोपाळगड) is a fort located 51 km (32 mi) from Chiplun, in Ratnagiri district, of Maharashtra.
overview.description: Gopalgad Fort / Anjanvel Fort (Marathi: अंजनवेल चा किल्ला/ गोपाळगड) is a fort located 51 km (32 mi) from Chiplun, in Ratnagiri district, of Maharashtra. This fort is an important fort in Ratnagiri district. The Fort is located on a prominent and commanding point for guarding the trade route along the Vashishti River, which runs till Govalkot near Chiplun and the Dabhol port which was a busy route in medieval times. It is a fort located on a hill near the sea.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Ratnagiri Gas and Power
topPlaces.description: Ratnagiri Gas and Power Private Limited (RGPPL) is a Subsidiary of NTPC Limited. The company was established as a JV between GAIL and NTPC to take over and revive the assets of the defunct Dabhol Power Company.

topPlaces.name: Dabhol Power Station
topPlaces.description: Dabhol Power Station is located near Anjanwel village in Ratnagiri district in Maharashtra, India, about 160 kilometres (99 mi) south of Mumbai. The power station was built by the Dabhol Power Company (DPC), which was a joint venture of Enron International, General Electric, Bechtel and Maharashtra Power Development Corporation.

topPlaces.name: Vashishti River
topPlaces.description: River Vashishti is one of the larger rivers in the Konkan coast of Maharashtra, India. The river begins in the Western Ghats and snakes its way westwards towards the Arabian Sea.

topPlaces.name: Anjanwel
topPlaces.description: Anjanwel is a small coastal town in Guhagar taluka, Ratnagiri district, in the Konkan region and administrative division of the Indian state of Maharashtra. It is located around 80 kilometres (50 mi) north of the district headquarters of Ratnagiri, 10 kilometres (6.2 mi) north of its taluka, and 200 kilometres (120 mi) south of Mumbai, the state capital.

topPlaces.name: Dabhol
topPlaces.description: Dabhol (Marathi pronunciation: [d̪aːbʱoɭ]), also known as Dabul, is a small seaport town in the Ratnagiri district of Maharashtra in India. It is located on the northern and southern sides of the Vashishti River that later flows by the town of Chiplun.

topPlaces.name: Onanvase
topPlaces.description: Onanvase is a small village in Ratnagiri district, Maharashtra state in Western India. The 2011 Census of India recorded a total of 858 residents in the village.

topPlaces.name: Gudaghe
topPlaces.description: Gudaghe is a small village in Dapoli Taluka of Ratnagiri District, Maharashtra state in Western India. The 2011 Census of India recorded a total of 687 residents in the village.

topPlaces.name: Durga Devi Temple, Guhagar
topPlaces.description: Durga Devi temple, Guhagar is a temple at Guhagar, a taluka in Ratnagiri district of Maharashtra state in India.

itinerary.title: Day 1 — Ratnagiri Gas and Power & around

items.note: Ratnagiri Gas and Power Private Limited (RGPPL) is a Subsidiary of NTPC Limited. The company was established as a JV between GAIL and NTPC to take over and revive the assets of the defunct Dabhol Power Company.

Dabhol Power Station is located near Anjanwel village in Ratnagiri district in Maharashtra, India, about 160 kilometres (99 mi) south of Mumbai. The power station was built by the Dabhol Power Company (DPC), which was a joint venture of Enron International, General Electric, Bechtel and Maharashtra Power Development Corporation.
River Vashishti is one of the larger rivers in the Konkan coast of Maharashtra, India. The river begins in the Western Ghats and snakes its way westwards towards the Arabian Sea.

Anjanwel is a small coastal town in Guhagar taluka, Ratnagiri district, in the Konkan region and administrative division of the Indian state of Maharashtra. It is located around 80 kilometres (50 mi) north of the district headquarters of Ratnagiri, 10 kilometres (6.2 mi) north of its taluka, and 200 kilometres (120 mi) south of Mumbai, the state capital.

Dabhol (Marathi pronunciation: [d̪aːbʱoɭ]), also known as Dabul, is a small seaport town in the Ratnagiri district of Maharashtra in India. It is located on the northern and southern sides of the Vashishti River that later flows by the town of Chiplun.
Onanvase is a small village in Ratnagiri district, Maharashtra state in Western India. The 2011 Census of India recorded a total of 858 residents in the village.
Gudaghe is a small village in Dapoli Taluka of Ratnagiri District, Maharashtra state in Western India. The 2011 Census of India recorded a total of 687 residents in the village.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Anjanvel Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Anjanvel Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Anjanvel Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Pune Airport (139 km) and the nearest railway station is Pune Junction (131 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Anjanvel Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Anjanvel Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Anjanvel Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Anjanvel Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Anjanvel Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Ratnagiri Gas and Power, Dabhol Power Station, Vashishti River, Anjanwel, Dabhol.
